results.question: Compare the NuScale reactor safety valve (RSV) design philosophy with conventional PWR safety valves.
results.expected_answer: NuScale has 2 RSVs on the RPV head with setpoints of 2075/2100 psia to prevent overpressurization. Conventional PWRs have pressurizer safety valves (PSVs) on the separate pressurizer with similar functions. NuScale RSVs discharge into the containment (CNV) rather than to a relief tank, and the vacuum CNV aids in condensing discharged steam.
results.agent_answer:
The NuScale RSV design philosophy emphasizes integration within the reactor pressure vessel, use of pilot-operated valves for precise setpoints (2075 psia ±1%, 2100 psia ±1%), and compliance with ASME BPVC Section III, whereas conventional PWR safety valves are typically spring-loaded, externally mounted, and less integrated; NuScale’s approach enhances reliability and reduces potential leak paths.  
(Source: nuscale_ch05_0008, nuscale_ch05_0009, Table 5.2-2, Figures 5.2-1 and 5.2-2)
